title: AOT backend large import graph fixup/branch-island limits
goal: From eshkol-agent 2026-06-29 handoff: compiling an integration unit that imports the full app.agent startup graph failed during eshkol-run --compile-only with `error: <unknown>:0: fixup value out of range`, left a 0-byte test object, and production eshkol-agent builds still warn that `_main` is larger than the max branch-island cluster size. Need a focused reduction for oversized import graphs and generated entry points, sourceful diagnostics for backend fixup failures, and atomic object publication so failed compile-only runs never leave empty objects.
acceptance: A reduced large-import AOT compile no longer hits `fixup value out of range`
Backend fixup/branch-island failures include source/module context where possible
Failed compile-only/object emission leaves no 0-byte object at the destination path
Large generated entry-point builds either link cleanly or use a documented code-splitting/link strategy
status_note: Recorded from /Users/tyr/Desktop/eshkol-agent/plans/note-for-eshkol-agent.md after ICC readiness stayed ready/100. PR #92 / f92ba110aac98cadb8dc0f7d1f206b828e73c6db merged atomic backend object publication so failed compile-only/object emission does not publish 0-byte requested objects. PR #98 / 0dd1d2c81f89360f5a508d2b5b281f40519bcde0 merged the dump-ast robustness slice: LET-family AST printer cases no longer fall through to call_op union fields, fixing the bogus huge LET argument count/SIGSEGV around exact-integer-sqrt. PR #99 / 3f92dc55cc3726105d72e9a7c91bdf545b4091ba merged the object-emission diagnostics slice: opt-in ESHKOL_AOT_PHASE_TRACE and ESHKOL_OBJECT_EMIT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS report pass_manager.run/addPassesToEmitFile phase, source, target, temp path, opt level, and module counters; reduced broad-import probes now exit 124 with precise ESH-0088 diagnostics instead of silent hangs. Follow-up reduction found smaller repros than full app.commands: `(require test-support.testing)` plus one of `services.plugins.plugin-loader`, `agent.compaction`, or `services.api-client`; all time out in pass_manager.run after O0 cleanup at about 1.0k definitions, ~40k globals, ~2.0M instructions. Root cause remains source-inlining broad module graphs, especially default stdlib/source requires; agent-side smallest semantic triggers are services.api-client and services.plugins.plugin-loader JSON/runtime boundary bodies. `tools_test-learning` / process-error optimizer containment no longer reproduces on current origin/master with #93 merged, including -O 1 compile+run evidence; no new task needed. PR #100 / 882b7ed95c52475440617073019ae29439ba36b0 merged as a partial pressure reduction: pooling repeated raw error C strings drops the plugin-loader IR from globals=40027/private-string-constants=38313 to globals=25760/private-string-constants=24046 while preserving source-span type errors. PR #101 is in review for declaration-front-end pruning: external definitions imported from precompiled modules now register type/declaration shape without strict-type body synthesis or nested/lambda body scans; verification includes a fixture precompiled module whose intentionally bad body is ignored in compile-only strict mode. This does not reduce the plugin-loader pass_manager counters. Remaining open ESH-0088 work is true extern-only precompiled declaration metadata, module object caching/source-inlining reduction for broad imports, and the broader branch-island/code-size strategy.
campaign_payoff: Keeps Eshkol usable for production-size agent applications instead of only focused unit graphs.
